tasks.title: Record the non-negotiable Songs rendering rules
acceptance_criteria: A repository-local specification states that Songs charts are chord-only.
The specification preserves alphaTab as the required score renderer.
The specification requires exactly four equal-width measures per system at every supported viewport.
The specification forbids notes, rests, track names, and any text to the left of a system.
The specification requires final-system padding with empty measures.
The specification excludes the Songs score surface from Design Agent authority.
done_definition: The specification is committed with the implementation sample.
The implementation and browser review use the same requirements.

tasks.title: Normalize source songs into four-measure chord systems
acceptance_criteria: Imported-score songs use the approved Song section and chord data as the rendering source rather than note tracks.
Every source measure appears once and in order.
The final system is padded with non-semantic empty measures until it contains four measures.
done_definition: The generated chart input contains a multiple of four measures.
Padding does not change the reported musical measure count.

epics.title: Render the canonical four-measure chord chart

tasks.title: Configure alphaTab to render chord-only systems
acceptance_criteria: All Songs detail routes remain on the alphaTab renderer and use Song chord data rather than imported note tracks.
All Songs detail routes render chord symbols without melody notes, accompaniment notes, rests, or tablature.
Track names and other left-side text are hidden for every song source.
No custom HTML, canvas, or replacement score grid is introduced.
Loading and error states remain visible and accessible.
done_definition: The representative imported song and generated songs use the same alphaTab chord-only rendering path.
No notes, tablature, or visible rests are present in representative rendered systems.

tasks.title: Enforce four full-width measures per system
acceptance_criteria: Desktop and mobile systems both contain exactly four equal-width measures.
Every system spans the full available score container width.
The last system is four measures wide even when the song ends with fewer source measures.
No avoidable horizontal overflow or clipping is introduced.
done_definition: Representative first, middle, and final systems pass browser inspection at 1440px and 390px.
System left edges align consistently without a label gutter.

epics.title: Verify the sample and repository gates

tasks.title: Run focused tests and repository checks
acceptance_criteria: Focused tests or deterministic inspection cover AlphaTex final padding.
Lint, TypeScript, production build, and Supervisor validation pass.
React and Next.js implementation guidance is reviewed after TSX changes.
done_definition: All applicable checks have recorded exit codes.

tasks.title: Capture responsive visual evidence
acceptance_criteria: Before and after evidence exists for desktop and 390px mobile.
Browser review checks representative first, middle, and final systems.
Browser review confirms no notes, rests, left-side labels, clipping, or framework overlays.
Browser review confirms exactly four measures per system and full available-width fit.
done_definition: Screenshot paths and measured browser evidence are recorded in verify.json.
The rendered result is ready for the user to judge as the canonical sample.

assumptions: The existing Song section and measure structures are the authoritative chord-chart source for every Songs entry.
Four measures per system applies to mobile as well as desktop, even when chord text must become more compact.
The score surface is governed by specs/songs-score-rendering.md rather than a design-rule-engine profile.
risks: Long chord symbols may collide at 390px when four measures are forced into one system.
The imported song's Song data may contain less harmonic detail than its MusicXML note asset.
alphaTab may require rendered SVG inspection to distinguish hidden rests from empty notation space.
Four compact measures on a 390px viewport may require alphaTab scale tuning without changing its current score design.
